title: Raw user prompt export via external OpenTelemetry
why_it_matters: Grok Build's user guide documents an external OpenTelemetry stream under the [telemetry] table that is independent of the product telemetry master switch and ships to the operator's own OTLP collector. Within that table, otel_log_user_prompts is the content gate for raw user prompts; the documented example sets otel_log_user_prompts = false. This rule observes only that boolean at user scope: it does not inspect otel_enabled, exporters, or endpoints, so an enabled flag has no effect while external OTEL remains off, and other configuration layers are not inspected.
unknown_subject: external OpenTelemetry raw prompt export posture

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config keeps raw user prompts redacted from external OpenTelemetry logs (telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts = false).

outcomes.when: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is explicitly true in the user-level config
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config explicitly opts in to exporting raw user prompts with external OpenTelemetry logs (telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts = true).

remediation.summary: Stop exporting raw user prompts via external OpenTelemetry.

outcomes.when: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective external OpenTelemetry raw prompt export posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is unset in the user-level config; the user guide example redacts raw prompts (false) and OSS types default the Option to None (off unless enabled), but uninspected CLI flags, environment variables, managed/requirements config, or other layers may determine the effective value.

outcomes.when: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is set to a value outside boolean true/false
outcomes.message: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Grok Build user guide — Configuration
sources.notes: In-tree user guide at OSS SOURCE_REV 124d85bc5dc6e7805560215fcc6d5413944920e1 (retrieved 2026-07-17). Documents external OTEL under [telemetry] with otel_log_user_prompts = false as the content gate. External OTEL is independent of the product telemetry toggle. Local posture only.

sources.title: Grok Build telemetry configuration types
sources.notes: OSS SOURCE_REV 124d85bc5dc6e7805560215fcc6d5413944920e1. TelemetryConfig.otel_log_user_prompts: Option<bool> external OTEL content gate; default None in Default impl.

limitations: Only the user-level GROK_HOME/config.toml is inspected; CLI flags, environment variables, managed/requirements config, and project-scoped config are not inspected.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective value for every invocation.
This rule does not inspect otel_enabled, otel_logs_exporter, otel_endpoint, or related exporter keys; the user guide describes external OTEL as an independent opt-in.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention of exported OTEL data — no local file can.
Auth/account state is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
telemetry.otel_log_user_prompts set to a value outside boolean true/false (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
